tools.name: ollama_list_models
tools.description: List all locally installed Ollama AI models with sizes, families, parameter counts, and quantization levels. Start here to discover available models before running chat, generate, or embed.

tools.name: ollama_show_model
tools.description: Show details of a specific Ollama model including parameters, prompt template, capabilities (vision, tools, thinking), and license. Returns rendered markdown. Use after list_models to inspect a model before using it.

model.description: Model name (e.g. 'gemma3', 'llama3.2:7b'). Use list_models to discover available names.

tools.name: ollama_pull_model
tools.description: Download and install an AI model from the Ollama model registry. May take minutes for large models — do not retry on timeout. Use model names like 'gemma3', 'llama3.2', 'qwen3'. Use list_models after to confirm the download completed.

model.description: Model name to download from registry (e.g. 'gemma3', 'llama3.2:7b', 'qwen3:14b')

tools.name: ollama_delete_model
tools.description: Permanently delete a locally installed Ollama model. This is irreversible — the model must be re-downloaded with pull_model to restore it. Use list_models first to verify the exact model name.

model.description: Exact model name to delete. Must match a name from list_models.

tools.name: ollama_copy_model
tools.description: Create a copy of an existing local Ollama model with a new name. Use before create_model to preserve the original when customizing.

source.description: Existing model name to copy from (must be installed locally)

tools.name: ollama_create_model
tools.description: Create a custom Ollama model from an existing base model. Embed a system prompt, override the prompt template, or change quantization. May take minutes for re-quantization — do not retry on timeout. Use show_model after to verify the result.

from.description: Base model to derive from (e.g. 'gemma3'). Must be installed locally.

model.description: Name for the new custom model

quantize.description: Quantization level to apply (e.g. 'q4_K_M', 'q8_0'). Re-quantizes the model weights.

system.description: System prompt to embed permanently in the model

template.description: Go template string for prompt formatting

tools.name: ollama_list_running
tools.description: List currently loaded and running Ollama models with VRAM usage, context window size, and automatic unload time. Use to check GPU memory pressure before loading additional models.

tools.name: ollama_get_version
tools.description: Get the Ollama server version. Use to verify the server is reachable and check compatibility.

tools.name: ollama_chat
tools.description: Send a multi-turn chat conversation to a local Ollama model and get a complete response. Preferred over generate for conversations — maintains message history with roles. Supports tool calling, structured JSON output, vision (images in messages), and thinking/reasoning mode. Returns the full response in one call (non-streaming).

format.description: Constrain output format: 'json' for freeform JSON, or a JSON schema object for structured output

keep_alive.description: Duration to keep model in memory after request: '5m', '1h', or 0 to unload immediately. Default: 5 minutes.

messages.description: Array of message objects. Each has 'role' ('system', 'user', 'assistant') and 'content' (string). For vision: add 'images' array with base64-encoded strings.

model.description: Model name (e.g. 'gemma3', 'llama3.2'). Must be installed — use list_models to check.

options.description: Model parameters object: temperature (0-2), top_k, top_p, seed, num_ctx (context window), num_predict (max tokens), etc.

think.description: Enable chain-of-thought reasoning: true, false, 'high', 'medium', or 'low'. Response includes a 'thinking' field when enabled.

tools.description: Array of tool/function definitions the model may call (OpenAI function-calling format)

tools.name: ollama_generate
tools.description: Generate text completion from a single prompt using a local Ollama model. Use for one-shot generation without conversation history — prefer chat for multi-turn dialogue. Supports vision (base64 images), fill-in-the-middle (prompt + suffix), structured JSON output, and thinking mode. Returns the full response in one call (non-streaming).

format.description: Constrain output format: 'json' for freeform JSON, or a JSON schema object for structured output

images.description: Array of base64-encoded images for multimodal/vision models (e.g. llava, gemma4)

keep_alive.description: Duration to keep model in memory after request: '5m', '1h', or 0 to unload immediately. Default: 5 minutes.

model.description: Model name (e.g. 'gemma3', 'llama3.2'). Must be installed — use list_models to check.

options.description: Model parameters object: temperature (0-2), top_k, top_p, seed, num_ctx (context window), num_predict (max tokens), etc.

raw.description: If true, prompt is sent directly without applying the model's chat template. Use for pre-formatted prompts only.

suffix.description: Text after the insertion point for fill-in-the-middle completion. Model generates text between prompt and suffix.

system.description: System prompt to prepend. Overrides any system prompt embedded in the model.

think.description: Enable chain-of-thought reasoning: true, false, 'high', 'medium', or 'low'. Response includes a 'thinking' field when enabled.

tools.name: ollama_embed
tools.description: Generate vector embeddings for text using a local Ollama embedding model. For semantic search, similarity matching, and RAG pipelines. Supports single string or batch (array of strings) input. Not all models support embeddings — use an embedding model like 'all-minilm' or 'nomic-embed-text'. Returns 400 error if the model lacks embedding support.

dimensions.description: Custom output vector dimensions. Only supported by some models.

input.description: Text to embed: a single string, or an array of strings for batch embedding

keep_alive.description: Duration to keep model in memory: '5m', '1h', or 0 to unload immediately

model.description: Embedding model name (e.g. 'all-minilm', 'nomic-embed-text'). Must support embeddings — general chat models will return an error.

truncate.description: Truncate input exceeding context window (default true). Set false to return an error instead of silently truncating.

server_url.description: URL of an ACP server to connect to directly (e.g. http://localhost:8199). Overrides server name lookup

tools.description: List all registered projects via gRPC ProjectService.ListProjects. Returns Project objects with name, repo, branch, and agent templates.

tools.name: agents_project_register
tools.description: Register a new project via gRPC ProjectService.RegisterProject. A project defines a git repo and agent templates for spawning.

agents.description: JSON array of AgentTemplate objects. Each has: name (required), command (required), port_env, capabilities (string array), a2a_card_config ({name, description, skills, input_modes, output_modes, streaming})

branch.description: Default branch for new workspaces (default: main)

repo.description: Path or URL to the git repository

tools.name: agents_project_unregister
tools.description: Unregister a project via gRPC ProjectService.UnregisterProject. Fails if active workspaces with running agents exist.

tools.name: agents_workspace_create
tools.description: Create a new workspace via gRPC WorkspaceService.CreateWorkspace. Creates a git worktree and optionally auto-spawns agents.

auto_agents.description: JSON array of template names to auto-spawn (e.g. ["crush", "reviewer"])

branch.description: Git branch override (defaults to project's default branch)

name.description: Unique workspace name (used as worktree branch name)

project.description: Project to create workspace from (must be registered)

tools.name: agents_workspace_list
tools.description: List workspaces via gRPC WorkspaceService.ListWorkspaces. Optionally filter by project or status.

status.description: Filter by status: active or inactive

tools.name: agents_workspace_get
tools.description: Get workspace details via gRPC WorkspaceService.GetWorkspace. Returns agents, directory path, and creation time.

tools.name: agents_workspace_destroy
tools.description: Destroy a workspace via gRPC WorkspaceService.DestroyWorkspace. Stops all agents, cancels working tasks, and optionally removes the worktree.

keep_worktree.description: If true, preserve the git worktree directory on disk (default: false)

tools.name: agents_agent_spawn
tools.description: Spawn a new A2A agent via gRPC AgentService.SpawnAgent. Returns AgentInstance with id, port, direct_url, proxy_url, and status.

env.description: JSON object of additional environment variables

name.description: Custom instance name (defaults to template name)

permission.description: Permission level: session, project, or admin

prompt.description: Initial prompt to send after agent reaches READY

template.description: Agent template name from the project

workspace.description: Workspace name to spawn the agent in

tools.name: agents_agent_list
tools.description: List agent instances via gRPC AgentService.ListAgents. Optionally filter by workspace, status, or template.

status.description: Filter by status: starting, ready, busy, error, stopping, stopped

tools.name: agents_agent_status
tools.description: Get agent status via gRPC AgentService.GetAgentStatus. Returns AgentInstance with resolved A2A AgentCard.

tools.name: agents_agent_stop
tools.description: Stop an agent via gRPC AgentService.StopAgent. Cancels working A2A tasks, sends SIGTERM, waits grace period, then SIGKILL.

grace_period_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait after SIGTERM before SIGKILL (default: 5000)

tools.name: agents_agent_restart
tools.description: Restart an agent via gRPC AgentService.RestartAgent. Stop + spawn with same config. proxy_url stays stable.

tools.name: agents_agent_message
tools.description: Send a message to an agent via gRPC AgentService.SendAgentMessage. When blocking=true (default), polls until the task completes. When blocking=false, returns the task immediately for async tracking via agents_agent_task_status.

blocking.description: If true (default), wait for response. If false, return immediately.

tools.name: agents_agent_task
tools.description: Create a task on an agent via gRPC AgentService.CreateAgentTask. Returns immediately with a Task for async tracking via agents_agent_task_status (never blocks on completion).

context_id.description: A2A context_id to continue a conversation

tools.name: agents_agent_task_status
tools.description: Get task status via gRPC AgentService.GetAgentTaskStatus. Returns Task with status, artifacts, and history.

history_length.description: Maximum number of recent messages to include (default: 10)

tools.name: agents_discover
tools.description: Discover agents via gRPC DiscoveryService.DiscoverAgents. Returns enriched AgentCards. Supports local/network scope and capability filtering.

urls.description: JSON array of base URLs to probe for AgentCards (network scope)

tools.name: agents_proxy_list
tools.description: List all A2A AgentCards via the ARP HTTP proxy at /a2a/agents. Returns cards for READY/BUSY agents with metadata.arp fields.

tools.name: agents_agent_card
tools.description: Get an enriched A2A AgentCard via the ARP HTTP proxy. Includes metadata.arp and supportedInterfaces pointing to the proxy.

tools.name: agents_proxy_send_message
tools.description: Send an A2A message through the ARP HTTP proxy at /a2a/agents/{id}/message:send. Routes by agent ID, name, or workspace/name.

tools.name: agents_proxy_get_task
tools.description: Get an A2A task via the ARP HTTP proxy.

tools.name: agents_proxy_cancel_task
tools.description: Cancel an A2A task via the ARP HTTP proxy.

tools.name: agents_route_message
tools.description: Route an A2A message by skill tags via the ARP HTTP proxy at /a2a/route/message:send. Finds best matching agent (prefers READY over BUSY).

tags.description: JSON array of skill tags to match (e.g. ["coding"])

tools.name: signoz_list_services
tools.description: List all application services and their metrics (latency, error rate, throughput). Start here for APM, service health monitoring, and performance debugging.

tools.name: signoz_get_service_overview
tools.description: Get detailed overview metrics for a specific service over a time range. Use after list_services for latency percentiles, error rates, and request counts.

tools.name: signoz_top_operations
tools.description: Get top operations for a service ranked by latency, error rate, or call count. Use after list_services to drill into hotspot endpoints and slow operations.

tools.name: signoz_top_level_operations
tools.description: Get top-level (entry point) operations across all services. Useful for finding the most called API endpoints and root spans.

tools.name: signoz_entry_point_operations
tools.description: Get entry point operations for a service (v2). Returns the first spans in a trace for each service.

tools.name: signoz_search_logs
tools.description: Search and filter log entries. Supports attribute filters (severity, service, body text), ordering, and pagination. Start here for log exploration, debugging, and error investigation.

filter.description: Single filter expression: key op value, e.g. "severity_text = 'ERROR'". Only one expression per call.

tools.name: signoz_search_traces
tools.description: Search distributed traces with filters. Find slow requests, errors, and specific operations across services. Start here for trace exploration and latency debugging.

filter.description: Single filter expression: key op value, e.g. "hasError = true". Only one expression per call; use service param for service filtering.

tools.name: signoz_get_trace
tools.description: Get all spans for a specific trace by its trace ID. Shows the full request path across services with timing breakdown. Use after search_traces.

tools.name: signoz_query_metrics
tools.description: Query time-series metrics data. Supports aggregation (avg, sum, min, max, count, rate, p50-p99), filtering, and group-by. Start here for metrics exploration, infrastructure monitoring, and custom dashboards.

aggregate_op.description: Aggregation: avg, sum, min, max, count, rate, p50, p75, p90, p95, p99 (default: avg)

filter.description: Single filter expression: key op value, e.g. "service_name = 'frontend'". Only one expression per call.

group_by.description: Comma-separated attribute keys to group by, e.g. 'service_name,host'

tools.name: signoz_list_dashboards
tools.description: List all dashboards. Start here for dashboard discovery and visualization management.

tools.name: signoz_get_dashboard
tools.description: Get a specific dashboard by ID including all panels and widget configurations. Use after list_dashboards.

tools.name: signoz_create_dashboard
tools.description: Create a new dashboard with title, description, and optional tags.

tools.name: signoz_update_dashboard
tools.description: Update an existing dashboard. Send dashboard content (title, widgets, layout, tags) or the full get_dashboard response — nesting is auto-corrected. The API wraps content automatically.

dashboard.description: Dashboard content object with title, widgets, layout, tags, variables

tools.name: signoz_delete_dashboard
tools.description: Delete a dashboard by ID.

tools.name: signoz_list_alerts
tools.description: List all alert rules including their current state (firing, inactive, pending). Start here for alert management and on-call monitoring.

tools.name: signoz_get_alert
tools.description: Get a specific alert rule by ID with full configuration and current state. Use after list_alerts.

tools.name: signoz_create_alert
tools.description: Create a new alert rule. Requires a full alert rule definition JSON body.

rule.description: Full alert rule definition JSON object

tools.name: signoz_update_alert
tools.description: Update an existing alert rule. Send the full rule object (get it first with get_alert).

rule.description: Full alert rule definition JSON object

tools.name: signoz_delete_alert
tools.description: Delete an alert rule by ID.

tools.name: signoz_list_saved_views
tools.description: List all saved explorer views for logs and traces. Use to find pre-configured query views.

tools.name: signoz_get_saved_view
tools.description: Get a specific saved view by ID with full query configuration. Use after list_saved_views.

tools.name: signoz_create_saved_view
tools.description: Create a new saved explorer view with query configuration.

tools.name: signoz_update_saved_view
tools.description: Update an existing saved view.

view.description: Updated saved view definition JSON object

tools.name: signoz_delete_saved_view
tools.description: Delete a saved view by ID.

tools.name: signoz_list_channels
tools.description: List all configured notification channels (Slack, email, PagerDuty, webhooks) for alerts.

tools.name: signoz_get_version
tools.description: Get SigNoz server version, edition (community/enterprise), and setup status.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetches the content of a URL and returns it as readable text. Use this to read documentation, API references, README files, changelogs, GitHub raw content, package docs, or any web page whose content you need to reason about. Returns extracted readable text, not raw HTML. Start here for web browsing, URL reading, and online documentation lookup.

url.description: The full URL to fetch (https only).

tools.name: nomad_list_jobs
tools.description: List all jobs in the Nomad cluster. Start here for workload orchestration, container scheduling, and discovering running services.

tools.name: nomad_get_job
tools.description: Get full specification and status of a specific Nomad job, including task groups, constraints, and resource requirements. Use after list_jobs.

tools.name: nomad_get_job_versions
tools.description: Get version history for a Nomad job. Shows previous configurations and when changes were made.

tools.name: nomad_register_job
tools.description: Register (create or update) a Nomad job. Accepts a full job specification as JSON.

job.description: Full job specification as JSON object

tools.name: nomad_stop_job
tools.description: Stop (deregister) a running Nomad job. All allocations will be stopped.

purge.description: Completely purge the job from the system (true/false, default: false)

tools.name: nomad_force_evaluate
tools.description: Force a new evaluation for a Nomad job, triggering rescheduling. Useful when allocations are unhealthy or stuck.

tools.name: nomad_list_allocations
tools.description: List all allocations across the Nomad cluster. Shows where tasks are placed and their health status.

tools.name: nomad_get_allocation
tools.description: Get details of a specific Nomad allocation, including task states, events, restart history, and resource usage.

tools.name: nomad_get_job_allocations
tools.description: List all allocations for a specific Nomad job. Shows placement, health, and status of each task instance.

tools.name: nomad_stop_allocation
tools.description: Stop a specific Nomad allocation. The scheduler may place a replacement depending on job configuration.

tools.name: nomad_restart_allocation
tools.description: Restart a task within a Nomad allocation. Optionally specify which task to restart.

task.description: Task name (optional, restarts all tasks if omitted)

tools.name: nomad_read_allocation_logs
tools.description: Read stdout or stderr logs from a task in a Nomad allocation. Use for debugging container and workload issues.

log_type.description: Log type: stdout or stderr (default: stdout)

offset.description: Byte offset to start reading from

origin.description: Log origin: start or end (default: end)

plain.description: Return plain text instead of JSON (true/false, default: true)

tools.name: nomad_list_nodes
tools.description: List all client nodes in the Nomad cluster. Shows node status, datacenter, drivers, and scheduling eligibility.

tools.name: nomad_get_node
tools.description: Get full details of a specific Nomad node, including attributes, resources, drivers, host volumes, and metadata.

tools.name: nomad_get_node_allocations
tools.description: List all allocations placed on a specific Nomad node. Shows what workloads are running on the node.

tools.name: nomad_drain_node
tools.description: Enable or disable drain mode on a Nomad node. Draining migrates all allocations off the node for maintenance.

deadline.description: Drain deadline duration (e.g., '1h', '30m'). Use -1 for no deadline

ignore_system_jobs.description: Skip draining system jobs (true/false, default: false)

tools.name: nomad_node_eligibility
tools.description: Toggle scheduling eligibility for a Nomad node. Ineligible nodes won't receive new allocations but keep existing ones.

tools.name: nomad_list_deployments
tools.description: List deployments across the Nomad cluster. Shows rolling update status, canary progress, and deployment health.

tools.name: nomad_get_deployment
tools.description: Get details of a specific Nomad deployment, including task group status, health, and canary information.

tools.name: nomad_promote_deployment
tools.description: Promote canary allocations in a Nomad deployment. Moves canaries to production after validation.

all.description: Promote all task groups (true/false, default: true)

groups.description: Comma-separated list of task groups to promote (alternative to all)

tools.name: nomad_fail_deployment
tools.description: Mark a Nomad deployment as failed, triggering automatic rollback to the previous job version.

tools.name: nomad_list_evaluations
tools.description: List evaluations in the Nomad scheduler queue. Shows scheduling decisions, blocked evaluations, and failures.

tools.name: nomad_list_services
tools.description: List all registered services in the Nomad service discovery catalog.

tools.name: nomad_get_agent_self
tools.description: Get the current Nomad agent's configuration, stats, and node information. Useful for diagnosing agent state.

tools.name: nomad_get_cluster_status
tools.description: Get Nomad cluster status including Raft leader address and peer list. Use for cluster health checks.

tools.name: nomad_gc
tools.description: Trigger garbage collection on the Nomad cluster to clean up dead allocations, evaluations, and deployments.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_get_app
tools.description: Get the authenticated GitHub App's metadata including name, permissions, and events. Start here for GitHub bot and app identity management.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_list_installations
tools.description: List all installations of a GitHub App across organizations and users. Shows where the bot is installed and its access scope.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_get_installation
tools.description: Get details of a specific GitHub App installation including target account, permissions, and repository access. Use after botidentity_gh_list_installations.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_create_install_token
tools.description: Create a short-lived installation access token for a GitHub App bot. Token grants the bot's permissions and expires in 1 hour. Use to authenticate bot API calls.

installation_id.description: Installation ID to create token for

permissions.description: JSON object of permission overrides e.g. {"contents":"read","issues":"write"} (optional, defaults to app permissions)

repositories.description: JSON array of repository names to scope the token to (optional, defaults to all)

tools.name: botidentity_gh_list_install_repos
tools.description: List repositories accessible to a GitHub App installation. Shows which repos the bot identity can access.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_add_install_repo
tools.description: Add a repository to a GitHub App installation. Grants the bot identity access to an additional repository.

repository_id.description: Repository ID to add (numeric ID, not name)

tools.name: botidentity_gh_remove_install_repo
tools.description: Remove a repository from a GitHub App installation. Revokes the bot identity's access to a repository.

repository_id.description: Repository ID to remove (numeric ID, not name)

tools.name: botidentity_gh_suspend_installation
tools.description: Suspend a GitHub App installation. Disables the bot identity without uninstalling. All API access and webhooks are paused.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_unsuspend_installation
tools.description: Unsuspend a GitHub App installation. Re-enables a previously suspended bot identity, restoring API access and webhooks.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_delete_installation
tools.description: Delete a GitHub App installation. Permanently removes the bot identity from an organization or user account. Irreversible.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_get_webhook_config
tools.description: Get the webhook configuration for a GitHub App. Shows delivery URL, content type, and SSL verification settings.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_update_webhook_config
tools.description: Update the webhook configuration for a GitHub App. Change the delivery URL, content type, secret, or SSL settings.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_list_webhook_deliveries
tools.description: List recent webhook deliveries for a GitHub App. Shows delivery status, response codes, and timing for debugging bot event handling.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_redeliver_webhook
tools.description: Redeliver a failed webhook for a GitHub App. Retry a specific delivery that the bot failed to process.

tools.name: botidentity_gh_create_app
tools.description: Create a GitHub App via the manifest flow. Starts a temporary local web server, opens a browser form that auto-submits to GitHub, waits for the user to approve, captures the redirect code, exchanges it for credentials, and stores everything in the bot inventory. All-in-one.

bot_id.description: Bot inventory ID to store credentials on (from botidentity_create_bot)

events.description: JSON array of webhook events e.g. ["push","pull_request"] (optional, sensible defaults provided)

org.description: Organization slug to create the app under (optional, defaults to personal account)

permissions.description: JSON object of permissions e.g. {"contents":"read","issues":"write"} (optional, sensible defaults provided)

tools.name: botidentity_slack_create_app
tools.description: Create a new Slack bot app from a manifest. Defines the bot's name, scopes, event subscriptions, slash commands, and OAuth settings. Start here for Slack bot identity creation.

manifest.description: JSON string of the Slack app manifest defining bot name, scopes, features, and settings

tools.name: botidentity_slack_update_app
tools.description: Update an existing Slack bot app's manifest. Modify the bot's name, scopes, event subscriptions, slash commands, or OAuth settings.

manifest.description: JSON string of the full updated manifest (must include all fields, not just changes)

tools.name: botidentity_slack_delete_app
tools.description: Permanently delete a Slack bot app. Removes the bot identity and revokes all tokens. Irreversible.

tools.name: botidentity_slack_validate_app
tools.description: Validate a Slack bot app manifest without creating or updating. Check for errors before applying changes to bot configuration.

app_id.description: Optional existing app ID to validate against

manifest.description: JSON string of the Slack app manifest to validate

tools.name: botidentity_slack_export_app
tools.description: Export the current manifest of an existing Slack bot app. Use to inspect a bot's configuration or as a template for creating new bots.

app_id.description: Slack app ID to export manifest from

tools.name: botidentity_slack_get_bot_info
tools.description: Get information about a Slack bot user including name, icons, and associated app. Use to verify bot identity details.

token.description: Bot or user token with users:read scope (uses configured slack_config_token if omitted)

tools.name: botidentity_slack_rotate_token
tools.description: Rotate the Slack app configuration token using the refresh token. Returns a new access token and refresh token. The old tokens are invalidated. Config tokens expire after 12 hours.

refresh_token.description: The xoxe refresh token (uses configured slack_refresh_token if omitted)

tools.name: botidentity_slack_set_bot_icon
tools.description: Set a Slack bot's profile photo from a file path or base64-encoded image. Requires a bot token with users.profile:write scope. Use after botidentity_generate_logo to apply the generated image.

image_base64.description: Base64-encoded PNG image data (alternative to path)

path.description: File path to a PNG image (e.g. from botidentity_generate_logo output). Preferred over image_base64.

token.description: Bot token with users.profile:write scope (uses configured slack_bot_token if omitted)

tools.name: botidentity_generate_logo
tools.description: Open an interactive logo generator in the browser. Preview logos, tweak the prompt, regenerate until satisfied, then confirm. Logo is compressed under 900KB and saved to ~/.config/switchboard/logos/<bot-id> when bot_id is provided. Start here for bot branding and avatar creation.

bot_id.description: Bot inventory ID — saves logo to config dir and updates inventory automatically

model_id.description: Bedrock model ID (default: stability.stable-image-core-v1:1). Alternatives: stability.stable-image-ultra-v1:1, stability.sd3-5-large-v1:0

prompt.description: Initial text prompt for logo generation

tools.name: botidentity_create_bot
tools.description: Create a new bot identity across GitHub and Slack. Attempts to create on both platforms, registers in local inventory, and returns next steps for setup (install URLs, tokens to provide). Start here for new bot creation.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the bot

slack_manifest.description: Custom Slack app manifest JSON (optional — a sensible default is generated from the bot name)

tools.name: botidentity_delete_bot
tools.description: Delete a bot identity. Removes the Slack app (if created), provides instructions for GitHub App deletion, and removes the local inventory entry.

tools.name: botidentity_inv_list
tools.description: List all bots in the local inventory. Shows each bot's enabled platforms, app IDs, and logo. Filter by platform.

platform.description: Filter by platform: 'github' or 'slack' (optional, lists all if omitted)

tools.name: botidentity_inv_get
tools.description: Get full details of a bot from the inventory including credentials (masked), platform status, and next steps for incomplete setup. Use after botidentity_inv_list.

tools.name: botidentity_inv_update
tools.description: Update a bot's profile in the inventory. Change name, logo, or platform-specific IDs.

github_app_id.description: GitHub App ID (also enables GitHub if not already)

slack_app_id.description: Slack App ID (also enables Slack if not already)

tools.name: botidentity_inv_set_cred
tools.description: Store a credential for a bot. Well-known keys (slack_bot_token, github_private_key, github_webhook_secret, github_client_id, github_client_secret, slack_webhook_url) are stored in platform-specific fields. Other keys go to the generic credentials map.

tools.name: botidentity_inv_delete_cred
tools.description: Remove a stored credential from a bot in the inventory.

tools.name: botidentity_inv_get_creds
tools.description: List all credentials stored for a bot (values masked). Use botidentity_inv_get_cred to reveal a specific value.

tools.name: botidentity_inv_get_cred
tools.description: Get the full unmasked value of a specific credential for a bot. Use after botidentity_inv_get_creds to identify the key.

tools.name: botidentity_inv_set_logo
tools.description: Set or update a bot's logo path in the inventory. Use after botidentity_generate_logo to associate the generated image.

tools.name: botidentity_inv_set_meta
tools.description: Set a metadata key-value pair on a bot in the inventory. Use for descriptions, environment, team ownership, or any custom labels.

tools.name: x_get_tweet
tools.description: Get a single tweet by ID. Returns full tweet object with text, author, metrics, and entities

tools.name: x_get_tweets
tools.description: Get multiple tweets by IDs (up to 100). Start here for bulk tweet lookups

tools.name: x_create_tweet
tools.description: Post a new tweet. Supports text, replies, quotes, and polls

tools.name: x_delete_tweet
tools.description: Delete a tweet by ID. Must be authored by the authenticated user

tools.name: x_search_recent
tools.description: Search tweets from the last 7 days. Start here for most tweet discovery workflows. Supports the full X search query syntax

query.description: Search query (required). Supports operators: from:, to:, is:retweet, has:media, has:links, lang:, -is:retweet, etc.

sort_order.description: Order of results: recency or relevancy

tools.name: x_search_all
tools.description: Full-archive search across all tweets (requires Pro tier or higher). Use x_search_recent for 7-day window instead

query.description: Search query (required). Same operators as x_search_recent

sort_order.description: Order of results: recency or relevancy

tools.name: x_get_tweet_count
tools.description: Get count of tweets matching a search query from the last 7 days. Useful for analytics without retrieving full tweets

granularity.description: Aggregation granularity: minute, hour, or day (default day)

tools.name: x_get_quote_tweets
tools.description: Get tweets that quote a specific tweet

id.description: Tweet ID to get quotes for

tools.name: x_hide_reply
tools.description: Hide a reply to one of your tweets

tools.name: x_unhide_reply
tools.description: Unhide a previously hidden reply

tools.name: x_get_user_tweets
tools.description: Get tweets posted by a specific user. Use user_id from x_get_user or x_get_user_by_username

user_id.description: User ID (not username — use x_get_user_by_username to resolve)

tools.name: x_get_user_mentions
tools.description: Get tweets mentioning a specific user

tools.name: x_get_home_timeline
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's home timeline (reverse chronological). Requires user context auth

tools.name: x_get_user
tools.description: Get a user by their numeric ID. Use x_get_user_by_username if you have a @handle instead

tools.name: x_get_user_by_username
tools.description: Get a user by @username. Preferred entry point when you know the handle. Returns user ID needed for other endpoints

tools.name: x_get_users
tools.description: Get multiple users by IDs (up to 100)

tools.name: x_search_users
tools.description: Search for users by name or username

tools.name: x_get_me
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's profile information

tools.name: x_get_following
tools.description: Get users that a user follows

tools.name: x_get_followers
tools.description: Get users who follow a user

tools.name: x_follow_user
tools.description: Follow a user. Uses the authenticated user's ID automatically

tools.name: x_unfollow_user
tools.description: Unfollow a user

tools.name: x_get_blocked
tools.description: Get users blocked by the authenticated user

tools.name: x_block_user
tools.description: Block a user

tools.name: x_unblock_user
tools.description: Unblock a user

tools.name: x_get_muted
tools.description: Get users muted by the authenticated user

tools.name: x_mute_user
tools.description: Mute a user

tools.name: x_unmute_user
tools.description: Unmute a user

tools.name: x_get_liking_users
tools.description: Get users who liked a specific tweet

tools.name: x_get_liked_tweets
tools.description: Get tweets liked by a specific user

tools.name: x_like_tweet
tools.description: Like a tweet

tools.name: x_unlike_tweet
tools.description: Unlike a previously liked tweet

tools.name: x_get_retweeters
tools.description: Get users who retweeted a specific tweet

tools.name: x_retweet
tools.description: Retweet a tweet

tools.name: x_unretweet
tools.description: Remove a retweet

tools.name: x_get_bookmarks
tools.description: Get tweets bookmarked by the authenticated user

tools.name: x_bookmark_tweet
tools.description: Bookmark a tweet

tools.name: x_remove_bookmark
tools.description: Remove a tweet from bookmarks

tweet_id.description: Tweet ID to remove from bookmarks

tools.name: x_get_list
tools.description: Get a list by ID

tools.name: x_get_owned_lists
tools.description: Get lists owned by a user

tools.name: x_create_list
tools.description: Create a new list

private.description: Whether list is private (true/false, default false)

tools.name: x_update_list
tools.description: Update a list's name, description, or privacy setting

tools.name: x_delete_list
tools.description: Delete a list. Must be owned by the authenticated user

tools.name: x_get_list_tweets
tools.description: Get tweets from a list's timeline

tools.name: x_get_list_members
tools.description: Get members of a list

tools.name: x_add_list_member
tools.description: Add a user to a list. Must own the list

tools.name: x_get_dm_conversation

participant_id.description: User ID of the other participant

tools.name: x_send_dm

tools.name: x_get_space
tools.description: Get details about a X Space by ID

tools.name: x_search_spaces
tools.description: Search for X Spaces by title

state.description: Filter by state: live, scheduled, or all (default all)

tools.name: x_get_usage
tools.description: Get API usage statistics for tweet endpoints. Track your consumption

days.description: Number of days to look back (1-90, default 7)

tools.name: switchboard_list_integrations
tools.description: List all registered integrations with their enabled/healthy status, tool counts, and credential keys. Start here for Switchboard self-management, configuration, and integration setup. Shows which integrations are configured, which need credentials, and which are healthy.

enabled_only.description: If true, only show enabled integrations (default: false).

tools.name: switchboard_get_integration
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific integration including credential keys (not values), tool list, enabled/healthy status, and configuration hints. Use after list_integrations to inspect a single integration before configuring it.

tools.name: switchboard_configure_integration
tools.description: Configure an integration by setting credentials and enabling or disabling it. Credentials are merged with existing values — send only the keys you want to update. Set enabled=false to disable an integration without removing its credentials.

credentials.description: JSON object of credential key-value pairs to set (merged with existing).

enabled.description: Whether to enable the integration after configuring (default: true).

tools.name: switchboard_check_health
tools.description: Check connectivity health of one or all enabled integrations. Returns healthy/unhealthy status for each integration by calling its health endpoint. Use to verify credentials work and upstream APIs are reachable.

name.description: Optional: specific integration to check. Omit to check all enabled integrations.

tools.name: switchboard_browse_plugins
tools.description: Browse available plugins from configured marketplace manifest sources. Shows plugin name, description, latest version, and whether it is already installed. Use before install_plugin to discover available extensions.

tools.name: switchboard_install_plugin
tools.description: Install a plugin from the marketplace by name or from a direct URL. Downloads the WASM module, verifies its checksum, and registers it. Requires a server restart to load the plugin. Use after browse_plugins.

name.description: Plugin name from the marketplace (mutually exclusive with url).

url.description: Direct URL to a .wasm file to install (mutually exclusive with name).

tools.name: switchboard_uninstall_plugin
tools.description: Uninstall a marketplace plugin by name. Removes the WASM file and deregisters it from config. Requires a server restart to take effect.

name.description: Name of the installed plugin to remove.

tools.name: switchboard_server_info
tools.description: Get Switchboard server information including version, enabled integration count, total tool count, marketplace status, and runtime metrics summary. Use for diagnostics, status checks, and understanding the current server state.
